title: Generate data/synthetic/narratives.jsonl via text_generator.py
description: Run generator/text_generator.py for all 1000 participants. Load DEEPSEEK_API_KEY from .env. Batch calls — never call per-participant in a loop. Verify: (1) narratives.jsonl contains 1000 records; (2) all word_count values in 200-400 range; (3) PersonaNarrative.embedding is None for all records; (4) semantic spot-check — price_lex narratives mention price/value, compensatory narratives mention thoroughness. Log generation stats via structlog. STOP if >10% of narratives fail word count check.

title: Write final context update and prototype retrospective
description: Final context update. (1) Run drift_check.py and fix all warnings; (2) Update CLAUDE.md current phase to 'Prototype Complete'; (3) Create or update .claude/context/data-contracts.md with final stable schemas; (4) Archive Phase 1 and 2a post-mortems are already committed — verify paths in CLAUDE.md; (5) Write a 200-word executive summary of prototype findings to .claude/context/prototype-summary.md — written for a non-technical stakeholder; (6) Final git commit: git add . && git commit -m 'chore: prototype complete — final context update'.

title: PRD success criteria validation — verify all 5 criteria from PRD section 12
description: Validate all 5 PRD success criteria from PRD.md section 12. For each criterion, state: PASS / PARTIAL / FAIL with evidence. Criteria: (1) Strategy recovery >85% from process trace embeddings alone; (2) Embedding space shows clear strategy-based geometry (UMAP); (3) Contrastive loss converges, intra < inter by margin >0.3; (4) Modality ablation shows each stream contributes meaningfully; (5) Conjoint-format traces map coherently into same embedding space. Write findings to .claude/context/prd-validation.md. STOP for human review.

For each scenario: (1) choice redistribution table; (2) which personas are most/least sensitive; (3) sanity check — do price-lexicographic personas show highest price sensitivity? Do brand-affect personas show highest brand removal sensitivity? Flag any result that contradicts expected persona behaviour as a validity concern.

title: Notebook: 03_fusion_validation.ipynb — strategy recovery, ablation, geometry
description: Notebook: notebooks/03_fusion_validation.ipynb. Produce: (1) strategy recovery comparison table — all 4 single modalities vs fused (bar chart); (2) ablation heatmap — 15 modality combinations vs accuracy; (3) modality importance ranking from logistic regression coefficients; (4) UMAP of fused embeddings coloured by persona; (5) confusion matrix for fused model. Pass criteria (from PRD §12): fused accuracy > best single modality; each modality contributes positively in ablation. STOP for human review before closing.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: Description pending — Step 9 will add notebook structure and required plots.

title: Implement evaluation/counterfactual.py — novel choice set and price shift simulation
description: Implement evaluation/counterfactual.py. Simulate 3 counterfactual scenarios using fused consumer embeddings: (1) Price shift — increase all price attributes by 20%; predict choice redistribution per persona type; (2) New entrant — add a new alternative with best-in-class attributes; predict which personas switch; (3) Brand removal — remove the dominant brand; predict substitution pattern. Each scenario: present modified choice set to twin, simulate acquisition sequence using trace encoder, predict final choice. Output: choice redistribution table per scenario per persona.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: Description pending — Step 9 will add simulation methodology.

title: Implement evaluation/geometry.py — UMAP of fused consumer embeddings
description: Implement evaluation/geometry.py. Generate UMAP 2D projection of fused [1000, 512] embeddings. Compute: (1) mean intra-persona distance; (2) mean inter-persona distance; (3) silhouette score per persona. Compare to Phase 2a single-modality UMAP results — fusion should show tighter clusters. Save UMAP coordinates to data/synthetic/umap_fused.json for notebook use.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov

title: Implement evaluation/ablation.py — modality dropout evaluation
description: Implement evaluation/ablation.py. Systematically zero out each modality and measure accuracy drop. Run all 15 combinations (4 modalities, all subsets of size 1-4). Output: modality importance ranking by accuracy delta when removed. Key question: does any modality hurt when added (negative contribution)? Log all combinations to MLflow.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: Description pending — Step 9 will add evaluation methodology.

title: Implement evaluation/strategy_recovery.py — full fusion strategy recovery
description: Implement evaluation/strategy_recovery.py. Takes fused embeddings + labels. Computes: (1) overall strategy recovery accuracy; (2) per-class accuracy; (3) confusion matrix; (4) comparison table against single-modality baselines from Phase 2a. Output as structured dict for notebook consumption. Acceptance: fused accuracy > best single modality (trace encoder at >85%).

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: Description pending — Step 9 will add evaluation methodology.

title: Implement fusion/train.py — stack encoder embeddings and train meta-learner
description: Implement fusion/train.py. Load all 4 encoder checkpoints from MLflow (best run per modality). Freeze all encoder weights. Generate embeddings for all 1000 participants. Stack to [1000, 512]. Apply modality dropout (p=0.2) during training of MLPFusion. Train/val split: same participant_id split used in encoder training. Log to MLflow with tag stage=fusion. Acceptance: strategy recovery on fused embeddings > best single-modality encoder.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov
comments.text: CLARITY REVIEW: Description pending — Step 9 will add full implementation requirements.

title: Implement fusion/meta_learner.py — logistic regression baseline
description: Implement fusion/meta_learner.py. Read fusion/SPEC.md first. Two classes required: (1) LogisticFusion — sklearn LogisticRegression wrapper that accepts 4 embedding arrays and concatenates them; (2) MLPFusion — 2-layer PyTorch MLP [512→256→7] with dropout and modality dropout during training. Both must accept partial modality inputs (missing modality replaced with zeros + missing mask). Log modality importance weights (logistic regression coefficients) to MLflow.

comments.author: michaelarutyunov

title: Write fusion/SPEC.md

STOP for human review before closing.

title: Write phase 1 post-mortem: errors, deviations, assumption validation, Beads workflow assessment
description:
Read .claude/context/phase1-postmortem.md does not exist yet — create it.
Read before writing: CLAUDE.md, generator/SPEC.md, 
.claude/agents/generator-specialist/AGENT.md,
git log --oneline, uv run pytest tests/generator/ -v

Structure:
## 1. What Was Built
Inventory of files created/modified. Deviations from planned repo structure.

## 2. Errors and Resolutions
Each significant error: what failed, root cause, resolution. 
Categorise as: syntax/import, logic, schema contract violation, API/dependency.

## 3. Deviations from SPEC
What was implemented differently from generator/SPEC.md or AGENT.md.
For each: what changed, why, risk to downstream encoder work.

## 4. Context Infrastructure Gaps
Which AGENT.md/SKILL.md/SPEC.md sections were insufficient or missing.
What had to be inferred. Propose minimum additions for each gap.

## 5. Beads Workflow Assessment
Which task descriptions were sufficient, which needed clarification.
Did dependency ordering hold. Where did progress stall and why.

## 6. Assumptions Validated / Invalidated
For each PRD/SPEC.md assumption this phase touched: confirmed, 
partially confirmed, or contradicted. Evidence only — no speculation.

## 7. Recommendations for Phase 2
Concrete changes to SPEC.md templates, agent specs, task descriptions,
or architecture — based on observed evidence only.

Commit only the postmortem:
git add .claude/context/phase1-postmortem.md && git commit -m docs:

title: Implement generator/pipeline.py
description: Implement generator/pipeline.py. Orchestrates all generators for N participants. Reads personas.yaml, samples PersonaConfig, calls all generators with same config instance, runs validate.py, writes 5 JSONL files to data/synthetic/. Log INFO every 100 participants via structlog. Acceptance: uv run python -m generator.pipeline --n 10 produces valid JSONL files with consistent persona_id across all modalities.

title: Implement generator/validate.py
description: Implement generator/validate.py. Read generator/SPEC.md validation rules section. Check all 5 cross-modal consistency rules. Log failures at WARNING via structlog — never raise exceptions. Return validation report per participant. Acceptance: price_lex persona fails price_consciousness check when deliberately misconfigured (regression test); pytest passes.

title: Implement generator/text_generator.py
description: Implement generator/text_generator.py. Primary: DeepSeek via OpenAI-compatible endpoint (DEEPSEEK_API_KEY). Fallback: Anthropic API (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY). Load keys via python-dotenv. Generate from PersonaConfig.narrative params. Target 250-350 words. PersonaNarrative.embedding must be None at generation. Batch all calls — never call inside trial loop. Acceptance: word_count in 200-400 range; embedding=None; pytest passes with mocked API.

title: Implement generator/psychographic_generator.py
description: Implement generator/psychographic_generator.py. Produces PsychographicVector from PersonaConfig.psychographic params. Add per-participant Gaussian noise around archetype values. Demographics sampled from NarrativeParams. Acceptance: all continuous fields in 0-1 range; decision_style_dominant is valid enum value; pytest passes.

title: Implement generator/transaction_simulator.py
description: Implement generator/transaction_simulator.py. Read generator/SPEC.md and .claude/agents/generator-specialist/AGENT.md. Must produce TransactionRecord instances from PersonaConfig.transactions params. price_paid_normalised is 0-1 percentile, never raw price. 12-month lookback. Acceptance: mean price_paid_normalised consistent with persona price_sensitivity; channel_mix distribution matches params; pytest passes.

title: Implement generator/trace_simulator.py
description: Implement generator/trace_simulator.py. Read generator/SPEC.md for dwell distributions, Payne Index formula, calibration targets, and fatigue/time-pressure rules. Must produce AcquisitionEvent and TrialRecord instances. Dwell times log-normal only. Payne Index computed per trial. Acceptance: simulated Payne Index and prop_cells_inspected within calibration ranges in SPEC.md for each archetype; pytest passes.

title: Implement generator/persona_sampler.py
description: Implement generator/persona_sampler.py. Read .claude/agents/generator-specialist/AGENT.md and generator/SPEC.md first. Must: load config/personas.yaml via pyyaml, instantiate PersonaConfig from archetype params with per-participant noise, accept archetype_id and random_seed args. Acceptance: sample_persona('price_lex', seed=42) returns valid PersonaConfig; pytest passes.

title: Implement schemas unit tests
description: Write unit tests for all schemas/ dataclasses. Read .claude/agents/schema-guardian/AGENT.md first. Test: frozen=True enforcement, field types, enum serialisation as .value, cross-field constraints (e.g. channel_mix sums to 1.0, attribute_weights sums to 1.0). Location: tests/schemas/. Acceptance: pytest passes with >80% coverage on schemas/.

title: Populate config/personas.yaml with 7 archetype definitions
description: Populate config/personas.yaml with all 7 archetype definitions (price_lex, quality_lex, compensatory, satisficer, brand_affect, low_involve, adaptive). Source: .claude/skills/personas-yaml/SKILL.md. Output must be valid YAML. Acceptance: all 7 archetypes present with complete StrategyParams, TransactionParams, PsychographicParams, NarrativeParams fields. STOP for human review before closing.

title: Phase 1: Synthetic Data Generator
description: All synthetic data generation components: personas, traces, transactions, psychographics, narratives, validation, and pipeline orchestration

title: Notebook: generator EDA and cross-modal consistency check
description: Notebook: notebooks/01_generator_eda.ipynb. Load all 5 JSONL files from data/synthetic/. Validate: (1) Payne Index distributions by archetype, (2) prop_cells_inspected by archetype, (3) price_paid_normalised by persona price_sensitivity, (4) psychographic score distributions, (5) narrative word counts. Flag any archetype whose trace stats fall outside SPEC.md calibration ranges. Human review of plots required before Phase 2 begins.
